then.verdict: Mexico: your US visa is the ticket
then.headline: No separate Mexican visa needed while your US visa is valid
why: Mexico exempts Indian citizens holding a valid US (or Canada/Japan/UK/Schengen) visa or residence permit — stays under 180 days.
You complete an arrival form (FMM); keep it until departure.
Air entry is the smoother, better-documented path; keep every stamp.
pros: Zero paperwork, zero fees, long allowed stay
cons: The exemption rides on your US visa's validity — an expired stamp closes it even if your US status is valid

worst_case.scenario: You fly with an expired visa stamp (valid status, expired sticker) and are denied boarding
worst_case.mitigation: Check the visa sticker's end date, not your I-94, before booking

sources.title: Embassy of India, Mexico City — travel notice

then.verdict: Canada: full visitor visa, no shortcuts
then.headline: Your H-1B changes where you apply, not whether
why: Indian citizens need a Temporary Resident Visa regardless of US status.
From the US you apply online via IRCC and give biometrics at a US VAC.
Processing times are volatile — link out, never trust stored numbers.
pros: Multi-year multiple-entry TRVs are commonly issued, so it's one application for many trips
cons: Biometrics appointment scarcity in some US cities
Processing can outlast your travel plans

worst_case.scenario: You book flights first, and the TRV takes longer than the trip date
worst_case.mitigation: Apply months before any concrete plan; the TRV is trip-independent

then.verdict: UK: visits need a visa; transit has two branches
then.headline: Airside transit with a valid US visa needs no DATV — landside is a different question
why: Indian nationals are on the UK's transit-visa (DATV) list.
Airside (no border crossing, onward same day, to/from qualifying routes): DATV-exempt with a valid US, Canada, Australia or NZ visa — verified in Home Office guidance.
Landside (<24–48h, crossing the border for bags/terminal change): stricter separate rules; other countries' e-visas don't count. Verify your exact routing.
pros: Cheap London layovers become possible on the airside branch
cons: Booking a bags-recheck itinerary by accident puts you in the landside branch unprepared

worst_case.scenario: Separate tickets force a landside bag recheck you didn't plan for, and you're denied boarding at origin
worst_case.mitigation: Book through-checked itineraries for airside transit, or verify landside eligibility before booking split tickets

then.verdict: Schengen: H-1B changes nothing — plan for the appointment, not the form
then.headline: Visa required; the real constraint is consular appointment lead time from the US
why: Apply to the country of main destination, at the consulate/VFS holding jurisdiction over your US state (Arkansas typically routes via Houston for several countries — verify per country).
Appointment slots in peak season book out 4–8+ weeks; the paperwork itself is routine.
Most consulates want your US status/visa valid months beyond your return.
pros: Multi-year multiple-entry Schengen visas increasingly issued to clean travel histories
cons: Jurisdiction rules are per-country and unforgiving — the wrong consulate refuses the file

worst_case.scenario: No appointment exists within your travel window
worst_case.mitigation: Book the visa appointment BEFORE the flights; check multiple consulates' calendars where your itinerary honestly allows a different main destination

then.verdict: The list your US visa actually unlocks
then.headline: A valid US visa or green card changes the answer for Indians in a dozen-plus countries
why: Confirmed-by-official-source today: Mexico (visa-free), Türkiye (conditional e-Visa), UK airside transit (DATV-exempt).
Same pattern, verify before booking: UAE (14-day visa on arrival), Georgia (visa-free 90 days), Albania, Serbia (visa-free with valid US visa), Panama, Costa Rica, Bahamas (arriving from the US).
Each destination is its own record with its own conditions — validity minimums, 'used at least once' clauses, arrival-route requirements. The grid computes your verdicts.
pros: Real weekend-trip optionality from any US city without consulate runs
cons: Every one of these rides on the US visa's validity — the sticker, not your status

worst_case.scenario: You plan a multi-country trip around an exemption that changed last quarter
worst_case.mitigation: Open each destination's record the week you book — amber badge means re-verify at the official source linked there

actions.label: Open the grid with your documents applied

sources.title: Per-destination official sources on each record
